title: ClientRequest
description:
Bidirectional control protocol — client-initiated requests.

Each variant carries a unique `method` string used on the wire. The method is the discriminator; params are the variant-specific payload.

See `event-system-design.md` §5.1 for the base variants and §5.4 for gap additions.

oneOf.description:
Resolve a pending MCP elicitation request. Counterpart to the
`ServerRequest` the agent sends when an MCP server needs
structured user input (form values, OAuth tokens, etc.).
See `event-system-design.md` §5.4.

Interrupt one in-process teammate's active turn without
stopping the teammate lifecycle.
Apply feature flag settings at runtime.

AgentColorName.description:
`AgentColorName`. Validated set; unknown values are dropped at parse
time with a warning so the runtime never sees an invalid color.

AgentInterruptCurrentWorkParams.description:
Params for `agent/interruptCurrentWork`.

Aborts the target teammate's current model/tool turn while keeping the
teammate process alive for later messages.

AgentMcpServerSpec.description:
One entry in `AgentDefinition.mcp_servers`:
either a `string` (reference to an existing MCP server config) or
an inline `{name: config}` mapping that stands up a dynamic
server scoped to this agent. Inline configs are stored as
`serde_json::Value` because the underlying `McpServerConfig`
shape lives in `coco-mcp` (a higher layer that depends on
`coco-types`); keeping it as opaque JSON avoids a back-edge.

anyOf.description: Reference an existing MCP server by name (`string` arm).

Inline definition `{name: config}` (TS record arm). The map
always carries exactly one entry — the server name → its
`McpServerConfig` JSON. TS validates this with Zod on load.

ApprovalResolveParams.description: The SDK is *resolving* a pending approval request, sent client→server.

content_blocks.description:
Optional content blocks (typically image attachments) the SDK client
wants attached to the next user message. Paste-image-during-
AskUserQuestion or attachments alongside `MCPTool` answers ride this
slot. Carried verbatim as `serde_json::Value` because the underlying
content block shape is provider-specific; consumers translate per
provider.

feedback.description: Optional feedback to inject back to the model.

permission_updates.description: Permission updates authorized by the user and persisted to rules.

resolution_detail.description: Trusted tool-specific approval metadata selected by the client.

updated_input.description:
Optional rewritten tool input the SDK client supplies at
approval time. When `Some`, the engine substitutes this for the
model-emitted input before invoking the tool. Used by
`AskUserQuestion` to ship user-selected `answers` (and optional
`annotations`) back into the tool's data envelope.

In-process equivalent is `coco_tool_runtime::ToolPermissionResolution.updated_input`
(TUI mode). Consumed by the SDK approval bridge in `coco-agent-host`.

ClientAgentDefinition.description:
Client-supplied custom subagent spec carried on `InitializeParams.agents`.

Wire-level DTO. **Distinct** from the internal [`crate::AgentDefinition`]
which is the resolved post-load representation merged from markdown /
plugin / client sources.

background.description: Run as a fire-and-forget background task when invoked.

criticalSystemReminder_EXPERIMENTAL.description:
Experimental critical system reminder appended to the system prompt.
Wire field name: `criticalSystemReminder_EXPERIMENTAL`.

description.description: Natural-language description shown in the AgentTool prompt list.

initial_prompt.description: Auto-submitted as the first user turn when this agent is the main thread.

max_turns.description: Hard ceiling on agentic turns before the agent stops.

mcp_servers.description: Per-agent MCP servers (`string` name-ref or inline `{name: config}` map).

memory.description: Auto-loading scope for agent memory files (`user` / `project` / `local`).

permission_mode.description: Permission mode override for tool executions.

skills.description: Skill names auto-loaded into the agent context.

tools.description: Allowed tool names. `None` inherits all parent tools.

ElicitationResolveParams.description:
Params for `elicitation/resolve`.

Sent client→server in response to a prior `ServerRequest` that
asked the client to collect structured input on behalf of an MCP
server (form values, OAuth tokens, etc.). The client populates
`values` with the user's input and sets `approved=true`, or sets
`approved=false` to reject the elicitation.

approved.description:
Whether the user approved the elicitation. If `false`, `values`
is ignored and the MCP server sees a rejection.

mcp_server_name.description:
Which MCP server the elicitation originated from. Echoed back
so the agent can route the resolution to the right connection.

request_id.description: Correlation id matching the `ServerRequest` the agent sent.

values.description:
The collected field values keyed by field name. Each value is
an opaque JSON payload so typed/untyped fields share the wire
format. Empty when `approved=false`.

ExitPlanChoice.description:
The user's response to an `ExitPlanMode` approval prompt.
The wire `value` strings are the single source of truth for the choice
echoed back through `PermissionDecision::Ask.choices` →
`PermissionResolutionDetail::ExitPlanMode.choice`. Owning the mapping here keeps
the producer (the TUI permission bridge, which builds the choice list) and
the consumer (`ExitPlanModeTool::execute`, which branches on the picked
value) from drifting apart.
Response value for the exit-plan-mode permission request. Values mirror
upstream 2.1.193's `buildExitPlanModeOptions`/`Tar` branches so TUI
choice logs and SDK surfaces stay source-compatible.

oneOf.description: Clear context, then implement in Auto mode.
Clear context, then implement with permissions bypassed.
Clear context, then implement auto-accepting edits.
Keep context; auto-accept edits (or bypass when the gate allows).

Reject the plan and stay in plan mode. Never reaches `execute` (the
TUI maps it to a denial) — carried so the bridge and the "is this a
rejection?" check share one wire constant.

GoalCreateParams.description:
`session/goal/create` parameters. Minimal control-plane surface; contract and
rich budget authoring beyond the autonomous-turn cap are future extensions.

GoalEditParams.description:
`session/goal/edit` parameters. `expected_spec_revision` is the optimistic
concurrency guard (design §9.1 invariant 2): the edit is rejected when it does
not match the current spec revision.

GoalStatusRequest.description:
The controlled status transition a `session/goal/setStatus` requests. Only
user-drivable transitions are exposed; terminal and verifier-owned statuses
are not (design §6, §11.3).

HookCallbackMatcher.description: Hook callback matcher with optional tool-name filter and callback IDs.

InitializeParams.description:
Sent once at session start for capability negotiation. Carries hooks,
client MCP servers, output format, system prompt, and agent definitions
so the agent can construct its registries before the first turn.

agents.description: Custom agent definitions keyed by name.

client_mcp_servers.description: Client-provided MCP server names (to skip env-configured ones).

hooks.description: Hook callbacks keyed by event type.

prompt_suggestions.description: Enable prompt suggestions in the output stream.

MemoryScope.description: Scope for agent memory persistence. Controls where MEMORY.md is stored/read.

ModelRole.description: Which purpose a model serves. Multiple roles can map to different models.

oneOf.description:
Plan-mode model. Used in two distinct contexts:
1. **Subagent role** — when the built-in Plan subagent spawns
(`SubagentType::Plan → ModelRole::Plan` in
`core/subagent/src/subagent_role.rs:34`), the spawn factory
resolves the client via `client_for_role(Plan)`.
2. **Main-session plan-mode swap** — when the leader enters
`PermissionMode::Plan`, the engine swaps the active client
to `client_for_role(Plan)` for the duration of plan mode.
`getRuntimeMainLoopModel`'s
`opusplan` → Opus alias swap.
coco-rs encodes this as a generic role slot so it works for
any provider, not just Anthropic.
Unconfigured `models.plan` falls back to Main's spec via the
chain in `runtime.rs:507`, and `client_for_role` short-circuits
to the cached Main `Arc` — both call sites degrade cleanly to
"no swap" without spurious cache breaks.

Forked-agent spawn (AgentTool / SkillTool). Distinct from
`Explore` — Explore is an investigative subagent type that
happens to often be a "small fast" model; Subagent is the
generic spawn role used by tools/Agent and the swarm runtime.

PermissionMode.description:
Permission mode (top-level because it's used by both message and permission modules).
Wire format is camelCase. The serde aliases on the drifting variants accept
legacy snake_case input so old session transcripts deserialize cleanly.

PermissionResolutionDetail.description: Tool-specific trusted metadata attached to a permission approval.

PermissionRuleSource.description: Source of a permission rule (ordered by priority: Session is most specific).

PermissionRuleValue.description:
Permission rule value — tool_pattern is a glob/wildcard expression.
Examples: "Read", "Bash(git *)", "mcp__slack__*", "*"

PermissionUpdateDestination.description:
Destination for persisting permission updates.
Persistable destinations (`User`/`Project`/`LocalSettings`) write to
disk; in-memory destinations (`Session`/`CliArg`/`Command`) live only
for the running session. same split as
`persistPermissionUpdates` in `PermissionUpdate.ts`.
`Command` is reserved for rules contributed by an invoked command or
skill's frontmatter (`allowed-tools:`).
`alwaysAllowRules.command` populated by `SkillTool` /
`createGetAppStateWithAllowedTools`.

oneOf.description:
Rules contributed by a command/skill frontmatter `allowed-tools`.
In-memory only; cleared on session end.

ProviderModelSelection.description:
Unresolved provider/model selection from user-facing config.
This intentionally does not include [`ProviderApi`]: config surfaces
write `provider/model_id`, and the runtime resolves `provider` through
the live provider catalog before constructing a runtime slot.

QueuedCommandEditImage.description: Image payload paired with a queued command edit restore.

insertion_offset.description: Byte position in `prompt` where the atomic image chip should be restored.

ReasoningEffort.description:
Reasoning effort. Ordered from "off" through numeric intensity.
Provider-agnostic — `thinking_convert` maps to per-provider wire shapes.

oneOf.description:
Explicit "thinking off". Convert layer skips typed-effort
emission; `level.options` may still carry an explicit-off
wire toggle (e.g. DeepSeek's `{"thinking":{"type":"disabled"}}`).

planModeInstructions.description:
Custom workflow body for the plan-mode system reminder, re-supplied for
the resumed session. Per-session execution policy carried on resume for
the same reason it moved to `session/start` (off `initialize`).

SessionStartParams.description:
Params for `session/start`.

The serialized wire form carries only per-session execution policy — never a
session identity or history. A remote caller therefore cannot name or resume
an existing session through start; the server mints the identity. Unknown
fields (including legacy `session_id`/`initial_messages`/`initial_prompt`)
are rejected as invalid params, not silently ignored.

json_schema.description:
JSON schema for structured output. When present, the session registers
the StructuredOutput tool and requires a structured final result. This is
the per-session home for structured output (it is not an `initialize`
capability).

planModeInstructions.description:
Custom workflow body for the plan-mode system reminder. Per-session
execution policy: it lives here on `session/start`, not on `initialize`
(which carries connection capabilities only).

after_seq.description: Last durable envelope sequence the client already has.

SessionTarget.description: Selects persisted or live session state.

model.description: None means revert to the default model.

SetPermissionModeParams.description:
Params for `control/setPermissionMode`.

The `ultraplan` field (CCR web-UI refinement flow) is intentionally
omitted — see CLAUDE.md "Plan Mode — Skip Ultraplan Only".

SetThinkingParams.description:
Params for `control/setThinking`.
Uses `ThinkingLevel` which includes effort level and per-provider options.

SubmittedComposer.description:
Lossless, payload-light description of a composer after it has been
resolved into the model-facing prompt. Paste payloads are addressed by
ranges in that prompt; image bytes remain in typed file parts.

ThinkingLevel.description:
Unified thinking configuration for all providers.

`effort` carries provider-agnostic intent — `Disable`, `Auto`, or
one of the numeric levels (`Minimal`..`XHigh`). Provider-specific
wire toggles (e.g. DeepSeek's `{"thinking":{"type":"enabled"}}`)
flow through `options` verbatim.

Semantic states:
  * `Disable` — explicit "thinking off"; emit explicit-off signals
    where the provider supports them, otherwise omit reasoning fields.
  * `Auto`    — "let the provider decide"; omit reasoning fields
    so the server-side default applies.
  * `Minimal`..`XHigh` — explicit numeric efforts; emitted via the
    provider's typed reasoning channel.

budget_tokens.description: Token budget — universal for budget-based providers.

effort.description: Reasoning effort — universal across all providers.

composer.description: Lossless atomic composer description relative to `prompt`.

images.description: Optional clipboard/paste images to include on the user message.

model_selection.description:
Optional explicit turn-scoped model selection. When absent, the session
model remains authoritative.

slash_metadata.description:
Optional slash-command metadata to prepend as a model-visible
attachment before the user prompt.

answer.description: The user's answer to the `AskUserQuestion` prompt.
